title: Special Topologies
date: 2026-03-26
categories: [Mathematics, Topology]
tags: []

---

## Definition
**(i)** Let $\mathcal{B} = \\{ (a, b) \mid a, b \in \mathbb{R} \\}$. Then the topology generated by $\mathbb{B}$ is called the ***standard(usual) topology*** on $\mathbb{R}$.

**(ii)** Let $\mathcal{B}' = \\{ [a, b) \mid a, b \in \mathbb{R} \\}$. Then the topology generated by $\mathbb{B}'$ is called the ***lower limit topology*** on $\mathbb{R}$. We denote $\mathbb{R} _{\ell} = (\mathbb{R}, \mathcal{T} _{\mathcal{B}'})$.

**(iii)** Let $\mathcal{B}'' = \\{ (a,b), (a, b) \setminus K \mid a, b \in \mathbb{R} \\}$ where $K = \left\\{  \frac{1}{n} \mid n \in \mathbb{N} \right\\}$. Then the topology generated by $\mathbb{B}''$ is called the ***$K$-topology*** on $\mathbb{R}$. We denote $\mathbb{R}_{K} = (\mathbb{R}, \mathcal{T} _{\mathcal{B}''})$.

---

## Theorem
**(i)** $\mathcal{T} \subsetneq \mathcal{T} _{\ell}$

**(ii)** $\mathcal{T} \subsetneq \mathcal{T} _{K}$

**(iii)** $\mathcal{T} _{\ell}$ and $\mathcal{T} _{K}$ are NOT comparable.
